rulings.Case Title: CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA) N.A. VS. JAMES D. VISHNIAKOFF
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 21, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFF CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA) N.A.'s HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION HEARING REQUIRED. Defendant to clarify the amount of his monthly gross income. Defendant's financial statement indicates that his monthly gross income is $2,870/month while his employer's return states that it is $4,290/month. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: DALE DUNCAN ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion For Charging Order, Assignment Order, Restraining Order, And For Appointment Of Receiver (On Judicial Foreclosure Of Property Held By Debtor Anne Kihagi'S Llc: Rennka Prop, Llc]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 21, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFFS DALE DUNCAN, MARTA MUNOZ MENDOZA'S Motion For Charging Order, Assignment Order, Restraining Order, And For Appointment Of Receiver (On Judicial Foreclosure Of Property Held By Debtor Anne Kihagi'S Llc: Rennka Prop, Llc] Judge Pro Tem Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Judge Pro Tem. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Judge Pro Tem will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Judge Pro Tem has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing continued to December 11, 2018 at 9:00 am in Dept. 301. The Court is concerned about the need to avoid inconsistent judicial recommendation/rulings since there are pending three motions on very similar issues. Specifically, a hearing was held on November 14, 2018 before JPT Cohen for which he is writing a report. The Court believes that any ruling on the motion scheduled for November 20th and 21st should await a ruling from the Court on the first motion of this kind filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcatalanotti@fmglaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES PITTS VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO INTERROGATORIES
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 21, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, CHARLES FOLGER'S MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO INTERROGATORIES Judge Pro Tem Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Judge Pro Tem. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Judge Pro Tem will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Judge Pro Tem has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant City and County of San Francisco's unopposed Motion to Compel responses to Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, and Requests for Production of Documents is GRANTED. Sanctions in the amount of $732 are awarded in favor of defendant. Verified, amended responses, responsive documents, and sanctions are due within two weeks of notice of this order. Defendant shall provide notice of this order by express mail. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcatalanotti@fmglaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ALMA PLACE VS. DANIEL PATTERSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Requiring Undertaking Of Costs In The Amount Of $65,290 Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 1030; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 21, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANTs DANIEL PATTERSON and MARY PATTERSON's Motion For Order Requiring Undertaking Of Costs In The Amount Of $65,290 Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 1030; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof Defendants' motion for order requiring undertaking is DENIED. Defendants fail to show a "reasonable possibility" that they will obtain a judgment in their favor. The moving papers focus on the obviousness of the alleged defective conditions rather than the defective conditions themselves. Plaintiff would likely establish liability given the bizarre design of the handrails. The handrails are some 60 inches high at the bottom of the stairs and 22 inches high at the top. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu
